hero_subtitle: SFTY connects KYC and KYB provider outputs into a unified onboarding workflow that preserves audit evidence and supports risk-based compliance decisions for digital asset businesses.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Onboarding slows as identity data fragments
problem_description: Compliance teams struggle with slow and manual onboarding when KYC and KYB checks remain siloed across multiple providers. This creates gaps in identity verification, inconsistent risk review and weak audit trails that challenge defensible compliance decisions.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY unifies identity, company, sanctions and ownership checks from multiple providers into a single structured onboarding workflow with risk-based routing, audit-ready evidence and consistent review tracking.
workflow_title: From onboarding checks to approval decisions
workflow_description: SFTY connects identity and corporate checks, screening results and risk reviews into structured cases that guide approval and preserve compliance evidence.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond KYC checks. One onboarding layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects multiple identity, company and screening provider outputs into structured workflows, cases, decisions and audit-ready evidence for VASP onboarding and due diligence.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports VASPs with structured onboarding that preserves audit-ready evidence and enabling secure deployment for bank due diligence and enterprise governance.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY support structured identity verification workflows
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how KYC and KYB outputs create onboarding cases
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how audit-ready evidence supports identity verification reviews
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe provider orchestration for risk-based KYC and KYB checks
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for VASP onboarding teams?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides VASPs with a centralized compliance layer to connect KYC, KYB, sanctions, and onboarding outputs into structured workflows with audit trails and decision records.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY structure identity verification workflows?
faq_2_answer: SFTY organizes identity verification steps into risk-based reviews, case creation, approval paths, and evidence collection, reducing manual work and improving traceability across onboarding processes.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit-ready KYC evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves KYC case history, internal decision logs, provider results and regulatory evidence packages to support audit readiness and simplify regulatory inspections.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY connect existing KYC and KYB providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes, SFTY integrates and orchestrates outputs from multiple KYC, KYB, sanctions and corporate verification providers into one compliance workflow without disrupting current tools.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace KYC and KYB providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace providers but connects their outputs into structured workflows, cases, evidence and reports for better compliance oversight.
final_description: Review how SFTY connects KYC and KYB provider outputs into structured workflows with audit-ready evidence and case management.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
footer_contact_title: For supervisory, compliance and deployment discussions.
footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
